intro: New York City's Department of Finance traces to 1801, when the City Council first appointed a Comptroller, and was formally established as a department in 1831 with the Comptroller at its head. Today DOF administers the city's tax and revenue laws, collecting roughly $49 billion annually and valuing more than 1.1 million taxable properties with a combined market value exceeding $1.6 trillion, while also managing the city's treasury and adjudicating parking violations through its Sheriff's Office.
